source: Ash question 2026-05-14 — 'use of AI for duplication problem?'
title: Add embedding-based semantic dedup at L2 write time
what_failed: Current L2 dedup is purely lexical: prefix match (first 120 chars, same category) plus Jaccard-style word overlap. Semantic paraphrases slip through. Examples: 'I like dogs' vs 'I'm a dog person' share zero stopword-stripped tokens; 'I study at Georgia State' vs 'I'm at GSU' share none either. Both pairs end up as duplicate L2 entries that pollute distillation output and L3 ambient context.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py::_is_l2_duplicate uses string prefix only. _check_l3_overlap_and_conflict uses Jaccard word overlap. Neither captures meaning.
why_likely: Phase 6 shipped lexical dedup as a simple correctness fix (T-026). Semantic dedup was deferred because it required an embedding model in the loop and the failure mode hadn't yet shown up at user-visible level.
suggested_fix: Hybrid embedding + LLM fallback. 1) Add `embedding` JSONB column to organized_memory (or store vectors in a sidecar table). 2) On L2 write: embed the new fact via Gemini text-embedding-004 (~$0.00002/call, already have GEMINI_API_KEY) OR OpenAI text-embedding-3-small. 3) Cosine-similarity against existing facts in the same category. 4) >=0.90 -> skip as duplicate. 5) 0.75-0.90 -> borderline; ask Claude Haiku 'is this a duplicate of <existing>?' to break the tie. 6) <0.75 -> write. ~80 lines of code. Estimated cost at current usage: ~$0.01/month.
